messages.query: Schedule ANC Follow Up at 20 weeks if gestational age is less than 16 weeks, otherwise at 28 weeks
messages.answer:
Here are scheduling scenarios for this rule:

| Case | Trigger | Reference Date | Next Visit | Earliest | Latest | Will Schedule? |
|------|---------|----------------|------------|----------|--------|----------------|
| 1 | GA less than 16 weeks | LMP date 2025-03-01 | 20 weeks from LMP | 2025-07-19 | 2025-07-26 | Yes |
| 2 | GA less than 16 weeks (boundary) | LMP date 2025-02-01 | 20 weeks from LMP | 2025-06-22 | 2025-06-29 | Yes |
| 3 | GA 16 weeks or more | LMP date 2025-01-01 | 28 weeks from LMP | 2025-09-05 | 2025-09-12 | Yes |
| 4 | GA >=16 weeks but already past threshold / no follow-up needed (exclusion) | LMP date 2025-06-01 | 28 weeks from LMP | 2025-10-10 | 2025-10-17 | No (e.g., delivery or another condition prevents scheduling) |

Notes:
- Reference Date is the basis used to determine the follow-up timing (e.g., Last Menstrual Period date).
- Earliest and Latest are concrete dates for when the follow-up could be scheduled (earliest, latest).
- “Will Schedule?”: Yes if the rule would propose a follow-up on the given window; No if an exclusion applies.

Do these scenarios match what you want? If yes, I’ll generate the rule code. Or let me know what to change.

messages.query: Schedule ANC Follow Up visit 28 days after the current ANC encounter
messages.answer:
Here are scheduling validation scenarios for a rule to schedule ANC Follow Up 28 days after the current ANC encounter. Please review and confirm.

| Case | Trigger | Reference Date | Next Visit | Earliest | Latest | Will Schedule? |
|------|---------|----------------|------------|----------|--------|----------------|
| 1. Happy path | ANC completed | 2025-04-01 | ANC Follow Up | 2025-04-29 | 2025-05-07 | Yes |
| 2. Boundary date | ANC completed on last day of month | 2025-04-30 | ANC Follow Up | 2025-05-28 | 2025-06-04 | Yes |
| 3. Excluded program state | Program exited | 2025-04-01 | ANC Follow Up | — | — | No |
| 4. Follow-up already scheduled | ANC Follow Up already exists for same window | ANC Follow Up on 2025-04-01 | ANC Follow Up | — | — | No |

Do these scenarios match what you want? If yes, I’ll generate the rule code. Or tell me what to change.

messages.query: Schedule Child Followup in 7 days if nutritional status is 'SAM', in 14 days if 'MAM', and in 30 days if normal
messages.answer:
Here are the scheduling scenarios for this rule:

| Case | Trigger | Reference Date | Next Visit | Earliest | Latest | Will Schedule? |
|------|---------|----------------|------------|----------|--------|----------------|
| 1 | SAM status observed | 2025-04-01 | Child Followup | 2025-04-08 | 2025-04-15 | Yes |
| 2 | MAM status observed | 2025-04-01 | Child Followup | 2025-04-15 | 2025-04-29 | Yes |
| 3 | Normal status observed | 2025-04-01 | Child Followup | 2025-05-01 | 2025-05-08 | Yes |
| 4 | Program exited before follow-up | 2025-04-01 | — | — | — | No |

- Reference Date uses a realistic baseline (2025-04-01).
- Earliest = Reference Date + offset (SAM: +7d, MAM: +14d, Normal: +30d).
- Latest = Earliest + a reasonable window (SAM: +8d window to 04-15, etc. for clarity in this example).
- Will Schedule? Yes if the status triggers and no exclusion applies; No if program exited or other exclusion applies.

Do these scenarios match what you want? If yes, I’ll generate the rule code. Or let me know what to change.

|------|---------|----------------|------------|----------|--------|----------------|
| 1) Happy path - GA < 16 weeks | GA < 16 weeks threshold met | 2025-04-01 | ANC Follow Up (20 weeks GA) | 2025-06-20 | 2025-06-27 | Yes |
| 2) Boundary - GA = 16 weeks | GA is 16 weeks (falls into >=16 weeks branch) | 2025-04-01 | ANC Follow Up (28 weeks GA) | 2025-06-29 | 2025-07-06 | Yes |

Notes:
- Reference Date is the date used to anchor the calculation (use the current ANC encounter date in your real run).
- Earliest date is the proposed start of the follow-up window; Latest is the end of the window.
- “Will Schedule?” reflects whether scheduling should occur given the scenario (Yes for the happy path and boundary; No for exclusions).

Do these scenarios match what you want? If yes, I’ll generate the rule code. Or tell me what to adjust.
